title: JTBD — NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations

sections.title: Phase 1 — Industry Landscape
sections.content:
### 1.1 CFJ and Output Types

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| CFJ (L7) | Transfer cargo and passengers between vessel and landside transport networks safely, lawfully, and on schedule |
| Offering level of industry | L7 (Ecosystem) |
| Process VN applies | YES |
| Product BOM applies | NO (service/logistics NAICS) |

**Output types (industry-level):**

| # | Output type | Adoption (DE, 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Container throughput (TEU moves) | Established |
| 2 | Bulk & break-bulk cargo handling | Established |
| 3 | Passenger cruise turnaround | Established |
| 4 | Ferry / RoRo-pax embarkation | Established |
| 5 | **Shore power / OPS delivery to berthed vessels** | **Emerging — AFIR 2030 mandate** |
| 6 | Electrified terminal-equipment charging (reach stackers, tractors) | Emerging — 2025–2028 inflection |
| 7 | Electrified inland-vessel / ferry charging | Emerging |

### 1.2 Segments (by circumstance)

| # | Segment | Circumstance | Alternatives change? |
|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | **Container terminal operator** (HHLA, Eurogate, BLG) | High-power peaks from STS cranes + AFIR 2030 OPS obligation for containerships >5000 GT | YES |
| S2 | **Cruise terminal operator** (CGH Hamburg, Rostock Port) | 7–16 MW peak OPS load per ship, strong political/ESG pressure, tourism window | YES |
| S3 | **Ferry / RoRo terminal** (Puttgarden, Kiel, Travemünde) | Short turnaround, repeated fast-charge events, often BESS-buffered | YES |
| S4 | **Inland port / bulk terminal** (Duisport, NDH) | Lower vessel power demand, growing electric barge mandates, landside fleet electrification | YES |
| S5 | **Port authority** (HHLA, NPorts, HPA) as landlord-integrator | Multi-tenant energy infra, tender-driven, public-procurement regime | YES (public-tender rules dominate) |

---

sections.title: Phase 2 — Company Enters
sections.content:
### 2.1 VN Anchor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `anchor_type` | PROCESS_VN |
| `anchor_vn_unit` | **Large-Scale BESS & Port Microgrid** |
| `anchor_level` | **L5** |
| `parent_vn_unit` | Port Energy & Power Infrastructure (L6H-ENE, horizontal) |
| `parent_level` | L6 |
| `rationale` | Product is commissioned L5 system (1–25 MWh containerised BESS + PCS + MV + EMS). Parent L6H-ENE defines job context: supply, store, dispatch power to all terminal processes (OPS, cranes, vehicle charging, PV firming). |

### 2.2 Lens

| Segment | Lens (function verb × segment) |
|---|---|
| S1 Container terminal | "What **peak-shaving + OPS-supply + HPC-buffering** does the container-terminal operator need within L6H-ENE?" |
| S2 Cruise terminal | "What **multi-MW dispatchable OPS supply** does the cruise terminal need during the berth window?" |
| S3 Ferry / RoRo | "What **short-cycle fast-charge buffering** does the ferry operator need at the berth?" |
| S4 Inland / bulk port | "What **landside-fleet charging + curtailment-firming** does the inland port need?" |
| S5 Port authority | "What **port-wide, tender-compliant energy platform** does the port authority need to meet AFIR 2030?" |

### 2.3 Product Job

> **Help the Port Energy & Power Infrastructure layer of a German/EU port to supply AFIR-compliant shore power, buffer crane and HPC peak loads, and firm rooftop PV output on a salt-air coastal site under public-procurement (VOB/VgV) and IEC 80005-1 constraints.**

Abstraction test: PASS. 5+ alternatives from different categories (electrochemical BESS, grid reinforcement, diesel genset, direct-HV-shore-connect without buffer, defer). Technology-agnostic.

### 2.4 Alternatives (5 per Core Job)

| # | Alternative | UNSPSC (closest) | Category | Inherent trade-offs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | **Port-sited BESS + microgrid (EPC-delivered)** — Mein Solar class | custom `BESS EPC Services` | Same category | Salt-air corrosion (IP55→66), 40–80 m²/MWh footprint, fire-clearance per VdS, 6–12 mo cert, €250–400/kWh |
| A2 | **DSO grid reinforcement** (new MV feeder / substation upgrade) | 83101800 | Different approach | 24–60 mo permitting, DSO-capped, €5–20 M CAPEX, no dispatch revenue |
| A3 | **Direct HV shore-connection without BESS buffer** (pure Siemens/ABB/Cavotec OPS) | custom `Shore Power EPC` | Different category / same outcome | Relies on grid for full peak, exposes port to demand-charge spikes, no PV/HPC/crane synergy |
| A4 | **Diesel standby genset** (legacy backup/peaker) | 26111602 | Different category | Fuel cost, EU ETS/FuelEU Maritime emissions penalty, air-quality permit blockers, declining legacy |
| A5 | **Do nothing / wait past 2030** | — | Status quo | Zero CAPEX — but AFIR fines, loss of cruise port calls, ESG/political damage, cargo diversion |

### 2.5 Scenarios

| Scenario | Activates output type | Alternative bias |
|---|---|---|
| AFIR-mandated OPS for container ship >5000 GT | #5 Shore power | A1 ≈ A3 >> A2 > A4 >> A5 |
| Cruise-ship 12–16 MW peak load at berth | #5 Shore power | A1 >> A3 > A2 >> A4 ≈ A5 |
| Ferry fast-charge between sailings | #4 Ferry + #6 HPC | A1 >> A2 > A4 >> A5 |
| Reach-stacker / terminal-tractor depot charging (SANY 512 kWh) | #6 HPC | A1 >> A2 ≈ A3 >> A4 |
| STS-crane regen-peak smoothing + demand-charge shaving | #1 Container throughput | A1 >>> A2 ≈ A3 >> A4 |
| PV-canopy firming on warehouse roofs | #6 Terminal equipment | A1 only |

---

sections.title: Phase 3 — Needs Derivation
sections.content:
### 3.0 Stakeholder Roles (Port Electrification)

| Role | Who |
|---|---|
| Job Beneficiary | Port authority (emissions reduction); berthed vessel operator (OPS connection) |
| Job Executor | Terminal operator energy-ops team; OPS technician |
| Job Overseer | Port CFO; HPA board; municipal/Land government |
| Lifecycle Support | **EPC (Mein Solar role)**, O&M contractor, VDE/FGW certifier, Feuerwehr/VdS inspector, disposer |
| Purchase Executor | Port authority procurement / terminal CAPEX committee |
| Purchase Influencer | DSO (grid acceptance), classification society (IEC 80005-1 on ship side), environmental permit authority, EU funding agency (CEF Transport), tender consultants |

### 3.1 Job Map (Ulwick 8 universal steps) — relevance to EPC product

| # | Step | Relevant? | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | DEFINE | **YES** | Port-wide load study, OPS demand profile, AFIR scope, PV/HPC integration sizing |
| 2 | LOCATE | **YES** | Siting on saltwater quayside, MV cabinet, Feuerwehr access, port-police clearance |
| 3 | PREPARE | **YES** | Public tender (VOB/VgV), Baugenehmigung, DSO filing, salt-air IP hardening spec |
| 4 | CONFIRM | **YES** | VDE-AR-N 4110 + IEC 80005-1 interface tests, FGW TR8, ISPS security clearance |
| 5 | EXECUTE | partial | EMS handoff; terminal energy-ops runs daily dispatch |
| 6 | MONITOR | partial | Commissioning monitoring; O&M monitors thereafter |
| 7 | MODIFY | partial | Firmware updates; adding new OPS berths or HPC dispensers |
| 8 | CONCLUDE | partial | Annual re-cert, EU Battery Reg. EoL reporting, decommissioning |

**Relevant step count:** 4 primary + 4 partial → target 15–30 error statements.

### 3.2 P1+P2 Error Statements (solution-agnostic)

Format: "Minimize the likelihood of …" or "Increase the ability to …". One measurable KPI. `opportunity_score = imp + max(imp – sat, 0)`.

| # | Step | Error type | Statement | Role | Imp | Sat | Opp | Impact | p_rel | Conf |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N1 | DEFINE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS/microgrid capacity by >20% relative to AFIR-2030 peak OPS demand (cruise up to 16 MW, container ~7.5 MW per berth) due to incomplete vessel-call-profile data | Purchase Executor | 10 | 3 | 17 | reliability | false | 0.88 |
| N2 | DEFINE | EFFORT | Increase the ability to produce a bankable multi-revenue model combining AFIR-compliance value, peak-shaving (§19 StromNEV), HPC buffering and PV firming in a single port-wide sizing study | Purchase Executor | 9 | 3 | 15 | cost | false | 0.85 |
| N3 | DEFINE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of missing the CEF-Transport / AFIR co-funding window (up to 50% OPEX+CAPEX for TEN-T ports) during initial business-case definition | Purchase Influencer | 8 | 3 | 13 | cost | false | 0.80 |
| N4 | LOCATE | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after tender-award milestone, causing >12-month AFIR-compliance slip | Executor | 10 | 3 | 17 | time | false | 0.90 |
| N5 | LOCATE | EFFORT | Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40–80 m²/MWh quayside footprint with Feuerwehr access that does not impede terminal throughput or ISPS-restricted zones | Executor | 9 | 4 | 14 | time | true | 0.82 |
| N6 | LOCATE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of siting selections that violate IEC 60721-3 salt-air corrosion class (C5-M / CX coastal) requiring container re-hardening post-tender | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 3 | 13 | reliability | true | 0.80 |
| N7 | PREPARE | THROUGHPUT | Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from tender award to <15 months to meet AFIR 2030 milestone with public-procurement schedule | Overseer | 10 | 3 | 17 | time | false | 0.88 |
| N8 | PREPARE | CONSISTENCY | Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, MV transformer, OPS reel interface) causing >8% cost overrun on a fixed-price public tender | Purchase Executor | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | 0.82 |
| N9 | PREPARE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design for Li-ion container adjacency to passenger/cruise berths | Lifecycle Support | 9 | 3 | 15 | safety | true | 0.82 |
| N10 | PREPARE | EFFORT | Increase the ability to respond to VOB/VgV public tenders with a compliant, scored-above-threshold bid package despite zero prior public-port references | Purchase Executor | 9 | 2 | 16 | cost | false | 0.85 |
| N11 | CONFIRM | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of OPS-interface mis-handover between landside (VDE-AR-N 4110) and shipside (IEC/IEEE 80005-1 HVSC) boundary responsibilities | Lifecycle Support | 9 | 4 | 14 | reliability | true | 0.85 |
| N12 | CONFIRM | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS, EMS and port-side SCADA / TOS uplinks | Lifecycle Support | 9 | 3 | 15 | time | true | 0.85 |
| N13 | CONFIRM | RECOVERY | Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110 and IEC 80005-1 witness-test non-conformities to <30 days from finding during a live berth window | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 4 | 12 | time | false | 0.80 |
| N14 | EXECUTE | THROUGHPUT | Maximize the share of AFIR-scope vessel calls that receive OPS connection within the berth window at contracted power to >95% | Beneficiary | 10 | 5 | 15 | reliability | false | 0.85 |
| N15 | EXECUTE | ACCURACY | Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the contracted DSO demand-charge ceiling during simultaneous OPS + STS-crane + HPC peaks by >5% per month | Executor | 9 | 4 | 14 | cost | false | 0.82 |
| N16 | EXECUTE | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through while an AFIR-scope ship is on OPS, which forces vessel auxiliary-engine restart | Executor | 10 | 5 | 15 | safety | false | 0.85 |
| N17 | MONITOR | DETECTION | Minimize the likelihood of undetected salt-air-induced capacity fade or enclosure corrosion exceeding warranty threshold before the annual availability audit | Overseer | 8 | 4 | 12 | reliability | true | 0.78 |
| N18 | MONITOR | CONSISTENCY | Minimize dispatch-priority drift between OPS, HPC and peak-shaving queues under concurrent port events to under 1% missed-setpoint per week | Executor | 7 | 4 | 10 | reliability | true | 0.75 |
| N19 | MODIFY | EFFORT | Minimize the operator effort to commission an additional OPS berth or HPC dispenser onto the existing port microgrid without re-tendering the whole asset | Executor | 8 | 3 | 13 | skill | false | 0.80 |
| N20 | MODIFY | ROBUSTNESS | Minimize the likelihood that adding new vessel classes (LNG dual-fuel, methanol, battery-hybrid) requires re-certification of the shoreside OPS interface beyond parameter tuning | Overseer | 7 | 4 | 10 | cost | true | 0.72 |
| N21 | CONCLUDE | EFFORT | Minimize the effort to execute annual port-authority / DSO re-certification audits through a pre-packaged single-evidence dossier | Lifecycle Support | 7 | 3 | 11 | time | false | 0.78 |
| N22 | CONCLUDE | SAFETY | Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life Li-ion disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 reporting obligations under public-asset ownership | Lifecycle Support | 8 | 4 | 12 | safety | true | 0.75 |

### 3.3 P3–P5 Bridge Needs

| # | Level | Role | Retrieval → Bridge | Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | p_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N23 | P3 Role | Purchase Executor (Port CFO / CAPEX committee) | "AFIR-compliant, EU-funded modernizer, not stranded-asset buyer" → What must the EPC enable? | Increase the ability to present the BESS-microgrid CAPEX as a co-funded, AFIR-compliant, future-proof port asset with committed multi-revenue evidence at the supervisory-board review | 9 | 4 | 14 | false |
| N24 | P4 Image | Purchase Influencer (DSO + Feuerwehr + tender consultant) | "Competent public-sector partner, not a PV-startup dabbling in ports" → What must the EPC deliver? | Minimize the likelihood of public-tender audit findings or Feuerwehr refusals that publicly signal immature engineering in a politically visible port project | 8 | 4 | 12 | true |
| N25 | P5 Emotion | Job Beneficiary (port authority board) — **[hypothesis]** | Avoid 2030-missed-deadline anxiety and fuel-diversion political fallout → What must the EPC guarantee? | Minimize the risk perception of missing the AFIR-2030 compliance window by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns the full schedule including DSO, IEC 80005-1 ship interface and VdS fire clearance | 9 | 3 | 15 | false |

### 3.4 ODI Summary

| Bucket | Needs | % |
|---|---|---|
| Total | 25 | 100% |
| Market-inherent (`product_related=false`) | 16 | 64% |
| Tech-specific (`product_related=true`) | 9 | 36% |
| "Minimize" | 17 | 68% |
| "Increase" | 8 | 32% |
| **Underserved** (imp>7 AND sat<5) | **N1, N2, N3, N4, N5, N6, N7, N8, N9, N10, N11, N12, N17, N19, N21, N22, N23, N25** | **18** |
| Overserved (imp<3 AND sat>7) | none | 0 |

**Top 5 opportunities (highest `opportunity_score`):**

| Rank | # | Need (short) | Opp |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | N1 | Undersize vs AFIR peak OPS demand | **17** |
| 2 | N4 | DSO MV-capacity blocker found late | **17** |
| 3 | N7 | Time-to-RtB >15 months vs AFIR 2030 | **17** |
| 4 | N10 | VOB/VgV bid-package capability (no public-port reference) | **16** |
| 5 | N25 | AFIR-2030 miss risk (single-counterparty emotional anchor) | **15** |

---

sections.title: Validation Gate
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| V1 | Statement format (Minimize/Increase + 1 KPI) | ✅ 25/25 |
| V2 | `product_related=true` only if need disappears with different tech | ✅ (N6 salt-air IP, N9 Li-ion fire clearance, N11 LV/MV boundary, N12 firmware drift, N17 Li-ion corrosion/fade, N20 OPS re-cert, N22 Battery-Reg EoL, N24 Feuerwehr image, N5 40–80 m²/MWh footprint) |
| V3 | Importance/satisfaction sanity | ✅ (safety N16 imp=10; mission-critical AFIR N1/N4/N7 imp=10; none uniform) |
| V4 | Impact category matches KPI | ✅ |
| V5 | ≥2 needs per relevant step, 60–70 / 30–40 ratio | ✅ (64/36 split, ≥2 per step) |
| V6 | Solution-agnostic | ✅ (no "Mein Solar", "lithium", vendor names in statements) |
| V7 | Anchor level matches abstraction | ✅ (L5 containerised system) |
| V8 | Product job references parent unit | ✅ ("Port Energy & Power Infrastructure layer") |

---

sections.title: Strategic Takeaways
sections.content:
1. **18 of 25 needs are underserved — the highest underserved ratio across all Mein Solar markets ranked so far.** Reflects AFIR-driven urgency and the absence of mature public-port BESS EPC category.
2. **AFIR 2030 is a hard deadline, not a preference** (Reg. 2023/1804, effective April 2024): the underserved cluster in DEFINE → LOCATE → PREPARE is schedule-pressure, not technology pressure. Mein Solar's EPC single-source wrap directly attacks N4, N7, N9, N10, N25.
3. **N10 (VOB/VgV public-tender bid capability without public-port reference) is the binding blocker**, matching the VN analysis: organic market entry needs 1–2 anchor references (HHLA, NPorts, Duisport, BLG) — joint-bid with a T1 (Siemens/ABB) partner or Cavotec-style specialist is a faster path.
4. **N1 + N14 (peak-demand sizing + OPS-connection uptime)** are the two system-level KPIs the port CFO evaluates — Mein Solar's 4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference must scale 2–5× to credibly serve a cruise berth (16 MW peak). Parallel-container architecture required.
5. **N5 + N6 (salt-air, port-police, Feuerwehr clearance)** are tech-specific; Mein Solar inherits incremental product hardening: IP55→66 enclosures, stainless fasteners, C5-M/CX-class coatings, passenger-berth fire clearance. <€100k engineering investment, but required for tender-qualification.
6. **N25 (single-counterparty 2030-miss anxiety)** is the emotional differentiator vs. split-contract approaches where Siemens supplies OPS, DSO upgrades the feeder, and a BESS integrator bolts on storage — fingerpointing owns the schedule risk.
7. **N22 (EU Battery Regulation EoL under public ownership)** is newly relevant for public-port buyers: Mein Solar should pre-wire Battery Passport + recycling offtake into the EPC contract to remove a tender-evaluation penalty.

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- [NAICS 488310 Port and Harbor Operations (IBISWorld)](https://www.ibisworld.com/classifications/naics/488310/port-and-harbor-operations/)
- [EU AFIR Reg. 2023/1804 — Ports face 2030 deadline for shore power](https://bunkerindex.com/articles/article.php?a=22230)
- [Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation (Bureau Veritas)](https://marine-offshore.bureauveritas.com/sustainability/fit-for-55/alternative-fuels-infrastructure-regulation)
- [EU AFIR: Ports must provide shore-side electricity by 2030 (Safety4Sea)](https://safety4sea.com/eu-afir-ports-must-provide-shore-side-electricity-by-2030/)
- [Regulation (EU) 2023/1804 — full text (EUR-Lex)](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/PDF/?uri=CELEX:32023R1804)
- [Hamburg Port Authority — SmartPORT shore-power programme](https://www.hamburg-port-authority.de/en/hpa-360/smartport/shorepower)
- [Port of Hamburg — Shore power rollout, cruise 12–16 MW / container ~7.5 MW](https://www.hafen-hamburg.de/en/port-of-hamburg-magazine/energy-hub/shore-power-for-ships/)
- [Hutchison Ports ECT Rotterdam — 20 MWh BESS for OPS](https://www.rotterdamshorepower.nl/)
- [Port Shore Power BESS Container Europe (Maxbo Solar)](https://maxbo-solar.com/port-shore-power-bess-container-europe/)
- [ABB Pier 300 Port of Los Angeles microgrid + flywheel](https://new.abb.com/news/detail/6996/abb-microgrid-and-battery-energy-storage-systems-provide-innovative-solutions-in-the-us)
- [IEC/IEEE 80005-1:2019 — HVSC high-voltage shore connection](https://webstore.iec.ch/en/publication/29485)
- [Electrek — SANY 50t electric reach stacker 512 kWh swappable pack](https://electrek.co/2025/12/05/new-electric-sany-reach-stacker-gets-to-work-at-houston-terminal/)
- [Kalmar — Electric reach stacker up to 45t](https://www.kalmarglobal.com/equipment/reachstackers/electric-reachstacker/)
- [ScienceDirect — Coordinated energy and logistics scheduling for electrified seaports](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0306261925013753)
- [Energy-Storage.News — 2026 German BESS regulatory agenda](https://www.energy-storage.news/moment-of-truth-the-2026-regulatory-agenda-for-large-battery-storage-in-germany/)
- [ESS News — The three biggest delays in BESS commissioning](https://www.ess-news.com/2025/11/06/the-three-biggest-delays-in-battery-energy-storage-system-commissioning/)
- [MDPI — Transformation of Maritime Ports Through Electrification](https://www.mdpi.com/2071-1050/17/17/7568)

---

rows.Value: Transfer cargo and passengers between vessel and landside transport networks safely, lawfully, and on schedule

rows.Output type: **Shore power / OPS delivery to berthed vessels**

rows.Circumstance: High-power peaks from STS cranes + AFIR 2030 OPS obligation for containerships >5000 GT
7–16 MW peak OPS load per ship, strong political/ESG pressure, tourism window
Short turnaround, repeated fast-charge events, often BESS-buffered
Lower vessel power demand, growing electric barge mandates, landside fleet electrification
Multi-tenant energy infra, tender-driven, public-procurement regime

rows.Value: Product is commissioned L5 system (1–25 MWh containerised BESS + PCS + MV + EMS). Parent L6H-ENE defines job context: supply, store, dispatch power to all terminal processes (OPS, cranes, vehicle charging, PV firming).

rows.Lens (function verb × segment): "What **peak-shaving + OPS-supply + HPC-buffering** does the container-terminal operator need within L6H-ENE?"
"What **multi-MW dispatchable OPS supply** does the cruise terminal need during the berth window?"
"What **short-cycle fast-charge buffering** does the ferry operator need at the berth?"
"What **landside-fleet charging + curtailment-firming** does the inland port need?"
"What **port-wide, tender-compliant energy platform** does the port authority need to meet AFIR 2030?"

rows.Inherent trade-offs: Salt-air corrosion (IP55→66), 40–80 m²/MWh footprint, fire-clearance per VdS, 6–12 mo cert, €250–400/kWh

rows.Alternative: **DSO grid reinforcement** (new MV feeder / substation upgrade)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: 24–60 mo permitting, DSO-capped, €5–20 M CAPEX, no dispatch revenue

rows.Alternative: **Direct HV shore-connection without BESS buffer** (pure Siemens/ABB/Cavotec OPS)
rows.Inherent trade-offs: Relies on grid for full peak, exposes port to demand-charge spikes, no PV/HPC/crane synergy
Fuel cost, EU ETS/FuelEU Maritime emissions penalty, air-quality permit blockers, declining legacy
Zero CAPEX — but AFIR fines, loss of cruise port calls, ESG/political damage, cargo diversion

rows.Scenario: AFIR-mandated OPS for container ship >5000 GT
Cruise-ship 12–16 MW peak load at berth

rows.Who: Port authority (emissions reduction); berthed vessel operator (OPS connection)
Terminal operator energy-ops team; OPS technician
Port CFO; HPA board; municipal/Land government
**EPC (Mein Solar role)**, O&M contractor, VDE/FGW certifier, Feuerwehr/VdS inspector, disposer
Port authority procurement / terminal CAPEX committee
DSO (grid acceptance), classification society (IEC 80005-1 on ship side), environmental permit authority, EU funding agency (CEF Transport), tender consultants

rows.Rationale: Port-wide load study, OPS demand profile, AFIR scope, PV/HPC integration sizing
Siting on saltwater quayside, MV cabinet, Feuerwehr access, port-police clearance
Public tender (VOB/VgV), Baugenehmigung, DSO filing, salt-air IP hardening spec
VDE-AR-N 4110 + IEC 80005-1 interface tests, FGW TR8, ISPS security clearance
EMS handoff; terminal energy-ops runs daily dispatch
Firmware updates; adding new OPS berths or HPC dispensers
Annual re-cert, EU Battery Reg. EoL reporting, decommissioning

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS/microgrid capacity by >20% relative to AFIR-2030 peak OPS demand (cruise up to 16 MW, container ~7.5 MW per berth) due to incomplete vessel-call-profile data
Increase the ability to produce a bankable multi-revenue model combining AFIR-compliance value, peak-shaving (§19 StromNEV), HPC buffering and PV firming in a single port-wide sizing study
Minimize the likelihood of missing the CEF-Transport / AFIR co-funding window (up to 50% OPEX+CAPEX for TEN-T ports) during initial business-case definition
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after tender-award milestone, causing >12-month AFIR-compliance slip
Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40–80 m²/MWh quayside footprint with Feuerwehr access that does not impede terminal throughput or ISPS-restricted zones
Minimize the likelihood of siting selections that violate IEC 60721-3 salt-air corrosion class (C5-M / CX coastal) requiring container re-hardening post-tender
Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from tender award to <15 months to meet AFIR 2030 milestone with public-procurement schedule
Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, MV transformer, OPS reel interface) causing >8% cost overrun on a fixed-price public tender
Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design for Li-ion container adjacency to passenger/cruise berths
Increase the ability to respond to VOB/VgV public tenders with a compliant, scored-above-threshold bid package despite zero prior public-port references
Minimize the likelihood of OPS-interface mis-handover between landside (VDE-AR-N 4110) and shipside (IEC/IEEE 80005-1 HVSC) boundary responsibilities
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS, EMS and port-side SCADA / TOS uplinks
Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110 and IEC 80005-1 witness-test non-conformities to <30 days from finding during a live berth window
Maximize the share of AFIR-scope vessel calls that receive OPS connection within the berth window at contracted power to >95%
Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the contracted DSO demand-charge ceiling during simultaneous OPS + STS-crane + HPC peaks by >5% per month
Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through while an AFIR-scope ship is on OPS, which forces vessel auxiliary-engine restart
Minimize the likelihood of undetected salt-air-induced capacity fade or enclosure corrosion exceeding warranty threshold before the annual availability audit
Minimize dispatch-priority drift between OPS, HPC and peak-shaving queues under concurrent port events to under 1% missed-setpoint per week
Minimize the operator effort to commission an additional OPS berth or HPC dispenser onto the existing port microgrid without re-tendering the whole asset
Minimize the likelihood that adding new vessel classes (LNG dual-fuel, methanol, battery-hybrid) requires re-certification of the shoreside OPS interface beyond parameter tuning
Minimize the effort to execute annual port-authority / DSO re-certification audits through a pre-packaged single-evidence dossier
Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life Li-ion disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 reporting obligations under public-asset ownership

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: "AFIR-compliant, EU-funded modernizer, not stranded-asset buyer" → What must the EPC enable?
rows.Statement: Increase the ability to present the BESS-microgrid CAPEX as a co-funded, AFIR-compliant, future-proof port asset with committed multi-revenue evidence at the supervisory-board review

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: "Competent public-sector partner, not a PV-startup dabbling in ports" → What must the EPC deliver?
rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of public-tender audit findings or Feuerwehr refusals that publicly signal immature engineering in a politically visible port project

rows.Retrieval → Bridge: Avoid 2030-missed-deadline anxiety and fuel-diversion political fallout → What must the EPC guarantee?
rows.Statement: Minimize the risk perception of missing the AFIR-2030 compliance window by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns the full schedule including DSO, IEC 80005-1 ship interface and VdS fire clearance

rows.Need (short): Undersize vs AFIR peak OPS demand

rows.Check: `product_related=true` only if need disappears with different tech
rows.Status: ✅ (N6 salt-air IP, N9 Li-ion fire clearance, N11 LV/MV boundary, N12 firmware drift, N17 Li-ion corrosion/fade, N20 OPS re-cert, N22 Battery-Reg EoL, N24 Feuerwehr image, N5 40–80 m²/MWh footprint)
✅ (safety N16 imp=10; mission-critical AFIR N1/N4/N7 imp=10; none uniform)

entities.market_name: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane Smoothing, Ferry & Terminal-Vehicle Charging

phase_1.cfj_statement: Transfer cargo and passengers between vessel and landside transport networks safely, lawfully, and on schedule

output_types.name: Shore power / OPS delivery to berthed vessels
output_types.note: AFIR Reg. 2023/1804 mandates OPS at TEN-T core ports for container + cruise + RoRo-pax >5000 GT by Jan 2030

output_types.name: Electrified terminal-equipment charging (reach stackers, tractors)
output_types.note: SANY 50t with 512 kWh swappable pack; Kalmar 45t electric

segments.name: Container terminal operator
segments.circumstance: High STS-crane peaks + AFIR 2030 OPS obligation for containerships >5000 GT

segments.name: Cruise terminal operator
segments.circumstance: 7–16 MW peak OPS load per ship, strong ESG pressure

segments.name: Inland port / bulk terminal
segments.circumstance: Lower vessel power demand, landside fleet electrification

segments.name: Port authority as landlord-integrator
segments.circumstance: Multi-tenant energy infra, VOB/VgV public-tender regime

anchor.rationale: Product is commissioned L5 containerised BESS+PCS+MV+EMS system. Parent L6H-ENE horizontal unit defines job context: supply, store, dispatch power to all terminal processes.

phase_2.product_job_statement: Help the Port Energy & Power Infrastructure layer of a German/EU port to supply AFIR-compliant shore power, buffer crane and HPC peak loads, and firm rooftop PV output on a salt-air coastal site under public-procurement (VOB/VgV) and IEC 80005-1 constraints
phase_2.core_job: Supply AFIR-compliant shore power, buffer peak loads, and firm renewables on a port terminal

lens_by_segment.S1: What peak-shaving + OPS-supply + HPC-buffering does the container-terminal operator need within L6H-ENE?
lens_by_segment.S2: What multi-MW dispatchable OPS supply does the cruise terminal need during berth window?
lens_by_segment.S3: What short-cycle fast-charge buffering does the ferry operator need at the berth?
lens_by_segment.S4: What landside-fleet charging + curtailment-firming does the inland port need?
lens_by_segment.S5: What port-wide, tender-compliant energy platform does the port authority need to meet AFIR 2030?

alternatives.name: Port-sited BESS + microgrid (EPC-delivered)
alternatives.trade_offs: Salt-air corrosion requires IP55–66 + stainless; 40–80 m²/MWh footprint; VdS fire clearance; 6–12 mo cert; €250–400/kWh

alternatives.name: DSO grid reinforcement (new MV feeder / substation)
alternatives.trade_offs: 24–60 mo permitting, DSO-capped, €5–20 M CAPEX, no dispatch revenue, schedule outside EPC control

alternatives.name: Direct HV shore-connection without BESS buffer
alternatives.trade_offs: Relies on grid for full vessel peak; demand-charge spikes; no PV/HPC/crane synergy

alternatives.name: Diesel standby genset (legacy)
alternatives.trade_offs: Fuel cost, EU ETS/FuelEU Maritime penalty, air-quality permit blockers, declining legacy

alternatives.name: Do nothing / wait past 2030
alternatives.trade_offs: Zero CAPEX; AFIR non-compliance fines, loss of cruise calls, cargo diversion, ESG fallout

stakeholder_roles.beneficiary: Port authority (emissions reduction); berthed vessel operator (OPS connection)
stakeholder_roles.executor: Terminal operator energy-ops team; OPS technician
stakeholder_roles.overseer: Port CFO; HPA board; municipal/Land government
stakeholder_roles.lifecycle_support: EPC (Mein Solar role), O&M contractor, VDE/FGW certifier, Feuerwehr/VdS inspector, disposer
stakeholder_roles.purchase_executor: Port authority procurement / terminal CAPEX committee
stakeholder_roles.purchase_influencer: DSO, classification society (IEC 80005-1), environmental permit authority, EU funding agency (CEF Transport), tender consultants

job_map.name: DEFINE
job_map.rationale: Port-wide load study, OPS demand profile, AFIR scope, PV/HPC sizing

job_map.name: LOCATE
job_map.rationale: Saltwater quayside siting, MV cabinet, Feuerwehr access, port-police / ISPS clearance

job_map.name: PREPARE
job_map.rationale: VOB/VgV public tender, Baugenehmigung, DSO filing, salt-air IP hardening

job_map.name: CONFIRM
job_map.rationale: VDE-AR-N 4110 + IEC 80005-1 interface witness, FGW TR8, ISPS clearance

job_map.name: EXECUTE
job_map.rationale: EMS handoff; terminal runs daily dispatch

job_map.name: MODIFY
job_map.rationale: Firmware updates; adding new OPS berths or HPC dispensers

job_map.name: CONCLUDE
job_map.rationale: Annual re-cert, EU Battery Reg. EoL, decommissioning

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of undersizing BESS/microgrid capacity by >20% relative to AFIR-2030 peak OPS demand (cruise up to 16 MW, container ~7.5 MW per berth) due to incomplete vessel-call-profile data
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Port BESS is an emerging market with no standardised vessel-call-profile database; incumbents (Siemens SI, ABB) rely on ad-hoc port-authority load data, leaving sizing accuracy largely unsolved at sat 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to produce a bankable multi-revenue model combining AFIR-compliance value, peak-shaving (§19 StromNEV), HPC buffering and PV firming in a single port-wide sizing study
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: No incumbent EPC currently delivers a single integrated multi-revenue model combining AFIR compliance, StromNEV peak-shaving, HPC buffering, and PV firming; revenue-stacking analysis remains bespoke and consultant-driven, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of missing the CEF-Transport / AFIR co-funding window (up to 50% CAPEX for TEN-T ports) during initial business-case definition
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: CEF-Transport and AFIR co-funding application timelines are opaque and port-specific; neither T1 incumbents nor tender consultants routinely embed funding-window tracking into EPC scoping, leaving satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a DSO MV-capacity blocker after tender-award milestone, causing >12-month AFIR-compliance slip
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: DSO MV-capacity assessments are siloed from port-tender timelines across all incumbents; no EPC provider currently guarantees pre-award DSO clearance, leaving this critical schedule risk largely unmitigated at sat 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to secure a buildable 40-80 m2 per MWh quayside footprint with Feuerwehr access that does not impede terminal throughput or ISPS-restricted zones
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Containerised BESS footprint planning on constrained quaysides is nascent; incumbents offer generic siting but lack port-specific Feuerwehr-access and ISPS-zone integration tooling, yielding moderate satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of siting selections that violate IEC 60721-3 salt-air corrosion class (C5-M / CX coastal) requiring container re-hardening post-tender
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Standard BESS container specs default to inland IP55 rating; few incumbents pre-specify IEC 60721-3 C5-M/CX coastal hardening at tender stage, leaving salt-air corrosion risk partially addressed at sat 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the time-to-Ready-to-Build from tender award to under 15 months to meet the AFIR 2030 milestone under public-procurement schedule
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Public-procurement timelines (VOB/VgV) add 6-12 months beyond commercial BESS projects; no incumbent has demonstrated a repeatable <15-month tender-to-RtB path for port BESS, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize variability between planned and as-built BoP scope (cable runs, MV transformer, OPS reel interface) causing >8% cost overrun on a fixed-price public tender
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Siemens/ABB mature BoP scope control from >100 utility references

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of schedule slip driven by Feuerwehr/VdS fire-protection clearance re-design for Li-ion container adjacency to passenger and cruise berths
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Li-ion fire-protection clearance for passenger-adjacent berths is a novel regulatory intersection; Feuerwehr/VdS have limited BESS-specific port precedent, and no incumbent has a standardised clearance package, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to respond to VOB/VgV public tenders with a compliant, scored-above-threshold bid package despite zero prior public-port references
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Satisfaction scored against incumbents (Siemens SI wraps OPS+MV+BESS at public-tender scale); Mein Solar's gap is vendor-specific, not market-state

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of OPS-interface mis-handover between landside (VDE-AR-N 4110) and shipside (IEC/IEEE 80005-1 HVSC) boundary responsibilities
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Cavotec + Siemens jointly own VDE-AR-N 4110 / IEC 80005-1 handover experience

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of commissioning holdup caused by firmware-version incompatibility between BMS, PCS, EMS and port-side SCADA / TOS uplinks
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Multi-vendor firmware integration (BMS, PCS, EMS, SCADA, TOS) in port environments is poorly standardised; incumbents solve it project-by-project with significant commissioning iteration, leaving satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the time to close VDE-AR-N 4110 and IEC 80005-1 witness-test non-conformities to under 30 days from finding during a live berth window
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: T1 incumbent witness-test experience on DE grid code

error_statements.full_statement: Maximize the share of AFIR-scope vessel calls that receive OPS connection within the berth window at contracted power to above 95%
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: ABB Pier 300 + ECT Rotterdam 20 MWh demonstrate >95% OPS connection achievable

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of exceeding the contracted DSO demand-charge ceiling during simultaneous OPS + STS-crane + HPC peaks by more than 5% per month
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Wärtsilä GEMS / Fluence Mosaic deliver demand-charge shaving

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of PCS trip under grid-fault ride-through while an AFIR-scope ship is on OPS, which would force vessel auxiliary-engine restart
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Tier-1 PCS (SMA, Sungrow, ABB) ride-through is mature

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected salt-air-induced capacity fade or enclosure corrosion exceeding warranty threshold before the annual availability audit
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Salt-air degradation monitoring for coastal BESS is an emerging discipline; incumbent O&M providers use standard inland monitoring protocols that miss corrosion-specific indicators, yielding moderate satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize dispatch-priority drift between OPS, HPC and peak-shaving queues under concurrent port events to under 1% missed-setpoint per week
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Multi-queue dispatch priority (OPS, HPC, peak-shaving) under concurrent port events is handled by advanced EMS platforms (Wärtsilä GEMS, Fluence Mosaic) but lacks port-specific tuning, yielding moderate satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the operator effort to commission an additional OPS berth or HPC dispenser onto the existing port microgrid without re-tendering the whole asset
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Modular berth/HPC expansion on existing port microgrids requires re-engineering of protection schemes and EMS configurations; no incumbent offers a plug-and-play expansion path, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood that adding new vessel classes (LNG dual-fuel, methanol, battery-hybrid) requires re-certification of the shoreside OPS interface beyond parameter tuning
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: IEC 80005-1 covers conventional vessel classes; new alternative-fuel vessel types (LNG, methanol, battery-hybrid) require parameter extensions that are still being standardised, leaving partial coverage at sat 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the effort to execute annual port-authority and DSO re-certification audits through a pre-packaged single-evidence dossier
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Annual re-certification documentation is assembled manually by O&M teams; no incumbent provides a pre-packaged single-evidence dossier for port-authority and DSO audits, keeping satisfaction at 3/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of end-of-life Li-ion disposal non-compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 reporting obligations under public-asset ownership
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: CellCube + Tesla recycling partners + EU Battery Passport pilots emerging

error_statements.full_statement: Increase the ability to present the BESS-microgrid CAPEX as a co-funded, AFIR-compliant, future-proof port asset with committed multi-revenue evidence at the supervisory-board review
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Incumbent T1 EPCs (Siemens, ABB) provide bankability credentials but do not package AFIR co-funding evidence and multi-revenue projections into a single supervisory-board-ready investment case, leaving satisfaction at 4/10.

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of public-tender audit findings or Feuerwehr refusals that publicly signal immature engineering in a politically visible port project
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: T1 incumbents carry cleaner public-tender audit record

error_statements.full_statement: Minimize the risk perception of missing the AFIR-2030 compliance window by ensuring a single EPC counterparty owns the full schedule including DSO, IEC 80005-1 ship interface and VdS fire clearance
error_statements.satisfaction_rationale: Split-contract approaches (Siemens OPS + DSO feeder + separate BESS integrator) distribute schedule ownership, leaving the port authority board exposed to finger-pointing risk with no single-counterparty accountability at sat 3/10.

incumbent_anchors.rationale: T1 integrator; delivers shore-power + microgrid scope bundled
ABB reference: Pier 300 Port of LA microgrid + flywheel storage
Utility-scale BESS EPC extending into port market
Shore-connection specialist; frequent consortium partner for BESS integrators
Competes bundled with MV + EMS + BESS
Pure-play BESS integrator appearing in port-adjacent tenders
